data.current_description: Initiates a payment transaction and generates a token for checkout processing.

data.current_description: Confirms payment status and retrieves transaction details for a given token.
data.description_suggestion: Verifies the status and retrieves complete details for a transaction token. Accepts a token identifier and returns a VerifyTokenResponse containing transaction status, payment details, customer information, and available payment options. Use this operation to check if a token is valid, pending, completed, or failed before processing charges or to retrieve transaction history.
data.reasoning: Original description is vague about what 'payment status' means and what specific details are returned. Added clarity on response contents (status states, payment details, customer info, payment options) and use cases (validation before charging, transaction history retrieval) to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'refundToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks specifics on what inputs are required (token, amount, reason), what is returned (refund confirmation, updated state), and side effects (transaction marked as refunded, funds returned). Added these details and clarified partial refund support for AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Processes a refund for a completed transaction.
data.description_suggestion: Processes a refund for a completed transaction. Accepts a RefundTokenRequest containing the token identifier, refund amount (full or partial), and optional reason. Returns a transaction result confirming the refund status and updated transaction state. The original transaction is marked as refunded and funds are returned to the customer's original payment method. Partial refunds are supported for transactions that support them.
data.reasoning: Description lacks specifics on what inputs are required (token, amount, reason), what is returned (refund confirmation, updated state), and side effects (transaction marked as refunded, funds returned). Added these details and clarified partial refund support for AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'cancelToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, missing_context. Description lacks clarity on what happens after cancellation (token becomes invalid), what is returned, and the distinction between cancellation and refunds. Added details about token invalidation, return value, and guidance on when to use cancel vs. refund for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Cancels a pending or authorized transaction.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels a pending or authorized transaction token, preventing further payment processing. Accepts a TokenRequest with the token identifier. Returns a transaction result confirming cancellation status. The token becomes invalid and cannot be used to process charges. This operation is typically used before payment completion; canceling a completed transaction requires a refund instead.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on what happens after cancellation (token becomes invalid), what is returned, and the distinction between cancellation and refunds. Added details about token invalidation, return value, and guidance on when to use cancel vs. refund for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'updateToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Description is vague about what 'transaction details' can be modified and when updates are allowed. Added specifics on updateable fields, state restrictions (pending only), return value, and use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to apply this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Modifies transaction details for a token before payment completion. Accepts an UpdateTokenRequest containing the token identifier and fields to update (e.g., amount, customer details, metadata). Returns the updated transaction details. Updates are only allowed on tokens in pending state; completed or failed transactions cannot be modified. Use this to correct transaction information or adjust amounts before charging.
data.reasoning: Description is vague about what 'transaction details' can be modified and when updates are allowed. Added specifics on updateable fields, state restrictions (pending only), return value, and use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to apply this operation.

data.current_description: Sends a payment link to a customer's email address.
